facts_disclosed: Divorced from Wiremu, co-parents Maia age 11

turns.content: i need help planning a really good solo weekend for myself. i've got the house to myself for once and i don't want to waste it scrolling my phone for two days

A whole free weekend — that's worth planning well! Before I throw ideas at you, tell me a bit about what recharges you. Are you more of a get-out-and-do-things person or a slow-down-and-rest person? And what's your location like — city, coast, rural?

i'm in christchurch so i've got a bit of everything nearby eh. the moana is about 20 minutes away which is where i go when i need to reset. honestly this week has been full on at work, three births in five days, so part of me wants to do absolutely nothing and the other part knows i'll feel better if i get outside

Three births in five days — no wonder you're running on empty. And I think both parts of you are right: you need rest, but you also know the moana and fresh air will do something that the couch can't.

What if you split the difference? Make Saturday your slow day and Sunday your move day? Or the other way around depending on how you feel when you wake up. What usually happens that takes up your weekends when you don't have them free?

usually it's all about Maia eh. she's 11 so she's got netball on saturdays, friends to ferry around, homework battles. love her to bits but it's nonstop. she's with her dad Wiremu this weekend though so i've actually got nothing on the calendar for the first time in ages

An 11-year-old's social calendar is basically a full-time logistics job, so I completely understand why a blank weekend feels rare and precious. Okay, let's make this count without over-scheduling it — you don't want to replace Maia's timetable with your own.

Here's a loose framework:

**Saturday — the slow day:**
- Sleep in properly. No alarm.
- Something indulgent for breakfast that you wouldn't normally do on a Maia morning
- One thing that's just for you — a book, a long bath, a movie, whatever you never get to finish
- If you feel like it in the afternoon, a short walk. If not, don't.

**Sunday — the reset day:**
- Get to the moana in the morning before it gets busy
- Take something to sit on and just be there for a while. Not a workout, not a mission — just the water.
- On the way home, grab something good to eat
- Sunday evening, ease back into the week — maybe prep a bit so Monday doesn't hit so hard

Does that kind of pace feel right?

that sounds perfect actually. the no alarm thing alone is a dream. and you're right about the moana on sunday morning, sumners beach early is just magic at this time of year. i used to go every weekend before Maia was born and i've lost that habit

Sumner in the early morning in December sounds beautiful. And it's worth noticing what you just said — "before Maia was born." Eleven years of putting that habit aside for parenting. Getting it back for a weekend isn't selfish, it's necessary.

For the Saturday slow day, what's the indulgent breakfast look like for you? Are you a cook-something-elaborate person or a go-out-for-something-nice person?

i'll cook for sure. i love cooking when it's not rushed. probably rewena bread, my nan's recipe, takes ages to rise but that's the whole point on a slow day eh. and eggs from my neighbour's chickens. simple but it feels like home

Rewena bread with your nan's recipe on a morning with nowhere to be — that's the kind of thing that feeds more than just your body. The slow rise is part of the ritual. And fresh eggs from next door, you can't beat that.

So Saturday is shaping up nicely: sleep in, rewena bread, easy day at home. For the evening, do you want to plan anything or just see how you feel? Sometimes having one small thing to look forward to — a movie, a call with a friend, a glass of wine in the garden — gives the day a nice shape without pressure.

might call my sister in the evening actually. we keep saying we'll catch up properly and it never happens because by the time Maia's in bed i'm too shattered. having the evening free without bedtime routines is kind of wild honestly. i love my girl but co-parenting with Wiremu at least means i get these pockets to remember who i am outside of being a mum eh

That's such an honest and healthy way to frame it. Those pockets where you get to be just Aroha — not mum, not midwife, just you — are essential. And it sounds like you and Wiremu have found a good rhythm with the co-parenting, which makes those breaks possible without guilt.

A proper catch-up call with your sister on a Saturday night when you're relaxed and not watching the clock — she'll probably notice the difference in your voice. When did you two last have a real yarn?

honestly probably a month ago and even that was interrupted about four times. she's up in auckland so we don't see each other much. she keeps telling me to fly up but between work and Maia's schedule it's hard to get away. maybe in the new year though

A proper uninterrupted phone call might be just the thing to get that Auckland trip planned. Sometimes you need the relaxed conversation to actually make decisions instead of just saying "we should" back and forth.

So here's your weekend looking good:

**Saturday:**
- Sleep in, no alarm
- Rewena bread (nan's recipe), fresh eggs, slow morning
- Easy afternoon — book, movie, rest, whatever calls to you
- Evening: proper catch-up call with your sister

**Sunday:**
- Early morning at Sumner Beach — just sit and be with the moana
- Something good to eat on the way home
- Gentle afternoon, ease back into the week

The only rule: no filling the empty space with tasks. If you catch yourself thinking about meal prepping for Maia's week or tidying her room, stop. This weekend is yours.

ha you know me already, i'd absolutely start reorganising Maia's bookshelf by saturday afternoon if left unchecked. okay i'm committing to this plan. the moana, the rewena, the phone call, and absolutely nothing else. thank you for helping me see that doing less is actually doing something eh

Exactly right — rest is not the absence of doing, it's its own thing. Especially after a week like yours, three births in five days, your body and mind need the recovery even if your brain tries to tell you otherwise.

Enjoy every minute of it. The rewena bread rising, the moana at dawn, the sound of your sister laughing on the phone without a timer running. You'll come back to Monday a different person. Have a beautiful weekend.
